notes: Tested against three hostile lenses. (1) 'This will be used to shame engineers out of legitimate burnout leave' — the falsifiability paragraph explicitly excludes medical leave, family leave, and planned leaves with clean handovers. The verse is restricted to 'mid-execution withdrawal in front of competent peers where the moment was visibly hard.' (2) 'No real engineer cares what other engineers think' — observably false; senior IC career trajectories are heavily mediated by peer-network signal, especially in the open-source-contributor subset. (3) 'The named tools (open-source Kubernetes operator, conference talks, GitHub stars) are decorative' — false, these are the actual reputation surface of the senior IC's career. The doctrinal claim — that competent peers update their model based on observed under-load conduct — is engineering-true and signal-theoretic. PASS.
